message:
feat(raptorq): add SymbolEquationArityMismatch validation to decoder pipeline

A received symbol whose columns and coefficients vectors have different
lengths indicates corrupted or malformed equation metadata. Previously
this mismatch silently produced incorrect sparse-matrix math in the
decoder, leading to garbage output or panics deep in the linear algebra
layer.

Add a new DecodeError::SymbolEquationArityMismatch variant that fires
early during input validation, before any matrix operations begin.
The existing duplicated validation logic in decode() and
decode_with_proof() is extracted into a shared validate_input() method
that includes the new arity check.

The proof pipeline gains a matching FailureReason::SymbolEquationArityMismatch
variant with a From impl so decode proofs capture the failure context.
The higher-level decode_block maps it to DecodingError::InconsistentMetadata
with a human-readable detail string including ESI, column count, and
coefficient count.

The pipeline E2E conformance test gains the missing match arm mapping
SymbolEquationArityMismatch to RejectReason::InconsistentEquations.

Tests: two new unit tests (decode and decode_with_proof paths) verify
the error variant is returned and the proof captures the correct
FailureReason.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
